titleInfo.title: Sutra on the Profound and Esoteric Liberation

mods.abstract: From cmuller, D. Lusthaus in the DDB (http://www.buddhism-dict.net.ubproxy.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/cgi-bin/xpr-ddb.pl?89.xml+id%28%27b89e3-6df1-5bc6-7d93%27%29): The [Saṃdhinirmocana-sūtra is the] single most important scriptural source for the doctrines of the Yogâcāra school.It is thought that the sutra was put together around 300 C.E. a little after the time of Nāgârjuna 龍 樹, during the middle period of Mahāyāna sūtras. Chinese translations include complete versions by Bodhiruci (深 密解脫經, 5 fasc. T 675.16.668-687; trans. in 514) and Xuanzang (解 深密經, 5 fascicles. T 676.16.688b-711b, trans. in 647), and partial versions by Guṇabhadra (相 續解脫地波羅蜜了義經 1 fasc. T 678.16.711-719; trans. between 435–443) and Paramârtha (佛 說解節經, 1 fasc. T 677.16.711-713; trans. in 557). There is no Sanskrit edition available, but there is a Tibetan translation, which is translated into English by John Powers. There is an English translation from the Chinese by John Keenan, and a French translation by Lamotte, based on Xuanzang and the Tibetan versions. References Keenan, John P. The Scripture on Explanation of the Underlying Meaning. Berkeley: Numata Center for Translation and Research, 2000. Powers, John. Wisdom of Buddha: The Saṃdhinirmocana Sūtra. Berkeley: Dharma Publishing, 1994. [cmuller, D. Lusthaus]

titleInfo.title: New edition of the Buddhist canon in the Taishō era
